biometric_trends_interpretation:confiden.josi_language: "With the data we have, it seems..."
"I don't have enough data to say for sure, but based on how you feel..."

biometric_trends_interpretation:readines.josi_action: watch closely, may need easier day
no concern, explain this is expected

biometric_trends_interpretation:trend_co.josi_action: mention what you can see, acknowledge gaps

chronotropic_viterbi_emission:chronotrop.notes: contribution to fatigue likelihood (0..1); at decoupling's level — an objective physiological signal, unlike the deliberately-lower subjective mental axis
semicolon-separated state:weight pairs (z-score means); max abs pull 0.6 = decoupling reference
require at least 3 eligible sessions with a valid suppression % in the window before contributing
suppression % mapped to z = 0 (0 % = at personal baseline)
suppression percentage-points per standard deviation in the z-map

coach_cues:default_focus_cue.cue: Execute with intention. Adjust if something feels off.

coach_cues:fatigue_notes.message: Time for reduced load — recovery drives adaptation.
Training load has been building — expect some fatigue.
Recent training has been light — room for more intensity.
Load is well-managed — good balance of stress and recovery.
Training has been repetitive — variety is important.
Listen to your body and adjust as needed.

coach_cues:feasibility_notes.note: Minor adjustments made for practical constraints.
Modified to fit available time or recovery.
Adapted due to time or fatigue constraints.

coach_cues:focus_cues.cue: Light movement only. Don't chase pace or power — this is about blood flow and recovery, nothing more.

Keep it genuinely easy. Smooth pedaling, relaxed grip, steady breathing through your nose. If you can't hold a full conversation, you're going too hard. This effort builds your aerobic engine — trust the process.

Find a rhythm you could hold for hours. Relax your shoulders, loosen your hands, and let your breathing stay controlled. Between efforts, keep the legs turning over lightly — active recovery flushes lactate and keeps you ready for the next block. The discipline of Z2 is staying easy when your legs say go harder.

This is your long ride — patience is everything. Stay in zone, fuel early and often, and hydrate every 20 minutes. Between efforts, spin easy and stay loose. If you drift above zone, dial it back — you'll thank yourself in the last hour.

Comfortably hard — you should feel purposeful effort but stay in control. Breathe rhythmically, keep your cadence steady, and focus on holding form. Between intervals, spin easy for the full rest period — don't cut it short.

Find your sustainable rhythm and lock in. This should feel like a pace you could hold for 30-40 minutes flat out — controlled discomfort. Stay relaxed in your upper body and let your legs do the work.

This is real work. Build into each interval over the first 30 seconds, then hold steady. Focus on controlled power, not surging. Between reps, spin easy and let your heart rate drop — the rest is part of the session, not wasted time.
Build effort progressively through each step. Recover fully between sets — walk or spin very easy. Quality deteriorates fast when you cut rest short.
Steady high effort. Find your threshold rhythm and hold it — this should feel like the hardest pace you could sustain for an hour. Focus on smooth power delivery.
Near-maximum effort. Commit fully to each rep and recover completely between them. If your form breaks down, the interval is done — stop and rest. Quality over quantity.
Explosive power — every rep should feel like a launch. Complete recovery between efforts is non-negotiable. If you're not fully recovered, wait longer.
Maximum speed and power. Every rep must be at full quality — the moment you can't hit the target, the set is over. Walk back recovery.
Absolute all-out effort for a few seconds only. Full recovery between reps — two to three minutes minimum. This is about neuromuscular recruitment, not fitness.

coach_cues:meso_phase_context.context: during ramp-in — building foundation before intensity
in the overload phase — expect to feel challenged
in a recovery week — lighter to allow adaptations
at steady state — maintaining without added stress

coach_cues:meso_transition_recommendatio.message: Illness risk: complete rest recommended until cleared
Focus on recovery: easy sessions only until state improves
Functional overreach detected: adaptation in progress, maintain course
Load above ceiling ({pct}%): consider reducing volume
Training on track: continue as planned

coach_cues:stimulus_cost_bands.message: Efficient training: strong adaptive signal with limited fatigue.
Balanced session: stimulus matches the cost.
This session costs more than it builds — that's intentional for durability.
Heavy session: significant fatigue investment for long-term gains.

coach_cues:variety_note.note: Session variety added to reduce repetitive stress.

coach_cues:zone_purpose.purpose_full: Active recovery — promotes blood flow without training stress
Base aerobic capacity — foundation for all other work
Aerobic efficiency — builds fat oxidation and endurance
Lactate clearance — improves ability to sustain moderate intensity
Lactate threshold — extends sustainable power at threshold
VO2max development — increases oxygen delivery to muscles
Anaerobic capacity — short, intense power development
Speed and power — neuromuscular recruitment and efficiency
Max power — peak force production and neural drive

cp_protocol:cp_fit_quality_gates.action_on_fail: reject fit; keep prior; surface "retest recommended"
reject fit; require more MMP data
flag profile as below trained range; advisor adjusts onboarding
flag profile as elite outlier; advisor verifies test
flag for review; W' below trained range
flag for review; W' above expected range

cp_protocol:cp_protocol_params.notes: new MMP > fitted curve by this % triggers refit
force refit at most every 8 weeks regardless
reject fits below this; advisor surfaces "retest" prompt
Bartram 2018 accelerated default (trained/elite); Skiba 2012 model τ is larger (~300–800 s); v1 learns per-athlete
minimum MMP points within fit window

cp_protocol:cp_refresh_triggers.condition: new MMP point > fitted curve by `refresh_breakthrough_pct` %

cp_protocol:cp_refresh_triggers.action: schedule retest in current/next build week
flag profile as low-confidence; surface advisor prompt

cp_protocol:cp_test_protocols.description: 12-, 7-, 3-min maximal time-trials, 30-min rest between, single session (Karsten 2017)
3-min step ramp to volitional failure (lab equivalent)
No formal test; CP fit from accumulated MMP records over 8 weeks

decoupling_policy:decoupling_eligibility.notes: reject if either half has < 10 min of valid HR
reject if > 15 % of HR samples are zero/invalid in a half

decoupling_policy:decoupling_sport_modif.rationale: running is more cardiovascular at same %CP/threshold; expect slightly higher drift

decoupling_policy:decoupling_thresholds.meaning: aerobic base solid for this duration/intensity
base nearing capacity for this work
base under-developed; reduce intensity, build volume
significant; advisor caps intensity, prioritizes Z2

decoupling_policy:decoupling_viterbi_emi.notes: require at least 2 eligible sessions in last 14d before contributing
rolling window for the per-state pull

energy_zones_v4:zone_breathing.breathing_cue: Natural, effortless breathing. Could hold a conversation or hum a tune.
Relaxed, rhythmic breathing. Nose breathing possible.
Controlled breathing, slightly deeper. Nose or mouth.
Deliberate breathing, mouth preferred. Notice the effort.
Deep, rhythmic mouth breathing. Controlled but working.
Heavy, rapid breathing. Cannot maintain a conversation.
Gasping for air. Maximum ventilation. Recovery breathing between reps.
Maximum ventilation. Brief maximal efforts. Full recovery between reps.
Breath-hold during effort, explosive recovery. Pure speed.

energy_zones_v4:zone_feel.feel_detailed: Barely any effort. Could do this all day. Recovery only.
Comfortable conversation possible. Building aerobic base.
Slight effort but sustainable for hours. Long sentences possible.
Purposeful effort. Short sentences only. Tempo feeling.
Few words only. Controlled discomfort. Threshold effort.
Cannot talk. Racing heart. VO2max effort.
Near-maximum power. Short bursts only. Anaerobic burn.
Peak power output. Seconds only. All-out effort.
Absolute max speed. 5-15 seconds only. Neuromuscular.

expression_catalog:expressions.instructions: By feel on terrain: surge {work_min} min strong uphill at 10k–5k effort, jog {rest_min} min easy. Repeat ~{reps}× until you have banked {ntiz} min of hard running. Same dose as the track set — the hill does the pacing.
expression_catalog:expressions.title: Hill Fartlek

expression_catalog:expressions.instructions: Find a steady climb: {reps}× {work_min} min hard uphill at 3k effort, walk/jog {rest_min} min down. {ntiz} min of VO₂ work total — let the gradient force the intensity.
expression_catalog:expressions.title: Hill Surge Session

expression_catalog:expressions.instructions: Continuous run finishing strong: start easy and wind the pace up so the final {ntiz} min sit at comfortably-hard tempo effort. No stopping — the progression IS the workout.
expression_catalog:expressions.title: Progression Run

expression_catalog:expressions.instructions: On a steady climb: {reps}× {work_min} min strong climbing at threshold effort, spin {rest_min} min easy back down. {ntiz} min of climbing work — same dose as the structured set.
expression_catalog:expressions.title: Climb Repeats

expression_catalog:expressions.instructions: Short climb or rolling terrain: {reps}× {work_min} min hard uphill efforts at VO₂ effort, {rest_min} min easy spinning between. {ntiz} min total above threshold.
expression_catalog:expressions.title: Hill Attacks

expression_catalog:expressions.instructions: Continuous ride building pressure: settle in easy, then hold the final {ntiz} min at steady tempo — smooth, controlled, no surges.
expression_catalog:expressions.title: Tempo Progression

fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.name: too_easy_rpe_gap
fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.notes: RPE 1.5+ below planned counts as "too easy" — increase load next meso (Foster 1998 sRPE adaptation window)

fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.name: too_hard_rpe_gap
fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.notes: RPE 1.5+ above planned counts as "too hard" — decrease load next meso (Foster 1998 sRPE adaptation window)

fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.name: low_compliance
fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.notes: Below 70% session-compliance triggers a load reduction — the athlete is missing sessions, the plan needs to meet them where they are

fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.name: high_delivery
fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.notes: Above 110% planned-vs-delivered ULS = athlete crushing it — increase load next meso (Bompa progressive-overload zone)

fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.name: low_delivery
fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.notes: Below 80% planned-vs-delivered ULS = athlete struggling — decrease load next meso

fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.name: max_increase
fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.notes: Hard ceiling on per-meso load increase (15%) — protects against unbounded ramp from compounding "too easy" signals (Issurin block-periodisation safety margin)

fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.name: max_decrease
fatigue_policy:transition_thresholds.notes: Hard floor on per-meso load decrease (20%) — even on a hard crash, deeper cuts are handled by `state_to_override` above, not by the transition multiplier (Meeusen 2013 overtraining recovery curve)

interval_generator_v1:patterns.description: Up then down: [w, w+Δ, ..., w+Δ, w]

interval_pattern_policy:fixed_protocol_v.rationale: Alactic recovery strides — 4–6×6s full recovery; fixed protocol so the 6s structure is never pattern-expanded or quantized (founder 2026-06-17)

josi_advisor_lines:advisory_offer_lines.template: You're fresh, so let's use it — {session}, about {duration_min}. This should feel good today.
Today's a good day for {session} — around {duration_min}. Settle in and enjoy the work.
Everything says go: {session}, {duration_min}. Have fun with it.
You're a touch tired, so {session} fits well today — about {duration_min} at a comfortable effort.
Middle sort of day, so we keep it sensible: {session}, around {duration_min}. Comfortable is the word.
{session} today — {duration_min}, nothing forced. It keeps you moving forward without digging in.
Your body wants an easier day, so the plan is {session} — a gentle {duration_min}. Think smooth, not strong.
We keep it light today: {session}, about {duration_min}. The point is to help recovery along, not to add load.
Less is the right call today. {session} for {duration_min} keeps you moving without digging the hole deeper.
Today is for backing off — if anything, {session}, a short {duration_min}, and only if it feels right.
Recovery leads today; {session} is the most that makes sense, about {duration_min}.
The suggestion stays minimal — {session}, {duration_min}, with rest as the real goal.

josi_advisor_lines:day_summary_lines.template: Rest day — nothing in the log, and that's exactly fine. This is where the training sinks in.
No sessions today. Good — your body uses days like this to get stronger.
An empty log today, and no guilt about it. Rest is part of the plan, not a break from it.
The day's work is in: {sport}, {duration_min} — banked and counted.
A focused day — {duration_min} of {sport} carried today's training.
Today came down to the {sport}: {duration_min}, logged and done.
That was a proper day of training — {activities_count} sessions and {duration_min} of work between them. Worth a good meal and an early night.
You stacked it up today — {activities_count} sessions, {duration_min} all told. Tomorrow will thank you for a calm evening.
A big day in the legs — {activities_count} sessions and {duration_min} of training. Let recovery do its part tonight.

josi_advisor_lines:headline_lines.template: You're looking fresh today — readiness is right up at {readiness_score}. Lovely day to do something with it.
Good place to be: {state}, readiness {readiness_score}. Enjoy this feeling — it's earned.
Readiness is up at {readiness_score} and you're {state}. If you've got plans today, your body's on board.
{state}, readiness {readiness_score} — you've got a green light today. Make it count.
You're {state} today — readiness {readiness_score}. There's room to work, just keep something in the tank.
Readiness reads {readiness_score} and you're {state}. A steady day suits you better than a hero day.
{state}, readiness {readiness_score} — nothing wrong with today, just don't force it.
Readiness sits at {readiness_score} and you're {state}. Train, but leave a little in reserve.
Readiness is down to {readiness_score} and you're {state}. Nothing to panic about — an easy day now buys you a better week.
You're {state} with readiness at {readiness_score}. Take the gentle option today; you've earned the break.
Readiness reads {readiness_score} today and you're {state}. Go easy — your next good session is behind a bit of rest.
{state}, readiness {readiness_score}. Today's job is simple: keep it light and let your body catch up.
Your body is asking for recovery — readiness is down at {readiness_score}. You can take today off with a clear conscience; this is how you come back strong.
Rest first today — readiness is at {readiness_score} and your body is clearly asking for a break. We pick it back up when you're ready.
Readiness has dropped to {readiness_score}, and that's your body being honest with you. Give it the day — sleep, food, feet up.
Today is a real rest day — readiness {readiness_score}. Nothing you'd gain today is worth the recovery you'd skip.
Recovery is paying off — your readiness is up at {readiness_score}.
Your body's bounced back nicely — readiness sits at {readiness_score}.
Your recovery is holding steady, with readiness at {readiness_score}.
Recovery is coming along — readiness at {readiness_score}, worth keeping an eye on.
Your fitness is carrying you well — readiness at {readiness_score}, {state}.
The training is banking fitness — you're {state} with readiness at {readiness_score}.
Fitness and fatigue are balancing out — readiness reads {readiness_score}.
The load is showing next to the fitness it builds; readiness at {readiness_score}.
Head and body agree today — you're {state}, readiness at {readiness_score}.
Good headspace on board, and readiness backs it up at {readiness_score}.
Your headspace deserves a look today; readiness reads {readiness_score}.
Mind a touch flatter than the body — readiness at {readiness_score}; keep the day kind.

josi_advisor_lines:no_data_line.template: We need more data to predict recovery.
We're still getting to know you — a few more days of data and the picture gets much clearer.
Not enough data yet to be honest about your recovery — and honest is the only way this works. A few more days fills it in.

josi_advisor_lines:supporting_clauses.clause_template: your body is still catching up
fitness and fatigue are about level
fatigue is outweighing fitness right now
and you're in a good frame of mind
and your headspace is worth a check-in
and your headspace deserves some care too

josi_advisor_lines:workout_reflection_li.template: That was a genuinely good {sport} — {duration_min}, and you nailed what it was meant to be. Days like this are where the fitness comes from.
Beautiful work out there — {duration_min} of {sport}, executed just right. Be proud of that.
That {sport} went exactly how you'd want it to — {duration_min}, controlled all the way. More of this and you're flying.
Solid {sport} today — {duration_min} of honest work. That's you moving forward.
Good session in the books: {duration_min} of {sport}. Keep stacking days like this and it adds up fast.
You did what today needed — {duration_min} of {sport}, steady and useful. Nicely done.
The {sport} got done — {duration_min} in the bank — even if it wasn't your smoothest. Those days count too.
A scrappy {duration_min} of {sport}, but you got through it. Not every session has to be pretty.
Today's {sport} wandered a bit from the plan — happens to everyone. The {duration_min} still counts.
That {sport} was harder than it should have been — it happens, and it says nothing about your fitness. {duration_min} logged; recover well tonight.
Rough session — the {duration_min} of {sport} didn't go to plan, and that's okay. The best answer is a good night's sleep, not a harder tomorrow.
Not your day out there, and that's allowed. {duration_min} of {sport} done; shake it off — form comes back quicker than you think.
That {sport} is in the book — {duration_min} of moving, and that always counts. There wasn't enough signal to say how it went, and I'd rather stay honest than guess.
Nice work getting the {sport} in — {duration_min} logged. Not enough came through to read the quality, so I'll leave it at: it happened, and it counts.
{duration_min} of {sport}, done and banked. I can't see enough to judge how it went — no guessing, just credit for showing up.

josi_explanations:density_rules.explanation: Structured intervals — use rest to maintain quality.
Significant rest periods — push hard in work segments.
Recovery-paced — effort in short bursts, long recoveries.

josi_explanations:fatigue_context_rules.explanation: Training load has been building — expect some fatigue.
Load is well-managed — good balance of stress and recovery.
Recent training has been light — room for more intensity.
Training has been repetitive — variety is important.
Cumulative stress is elevated — listen to your body.
Time for reduced load — recovery drives adaptation.

josi_explanations:feasibility_rules.explanation: Good session — minor adjustments for practical constraints.
Adapted session — modified to fit available time or recovery.
Minimal session — constrained by time or fatigue.

josi_explanations:focus_cue_rules.focus_cue: Maintain easy conversation pace. Stay relaxed.
Steady effort. Hydrate and fuel as needed.
Strong but controlled. Hold form through intervals.
Build effort progressively, recover between sets.
Near-max effort. Full recovery between reps.
Light movement. Don't chase pace or power.

josi_explanations:meso_phase_rules.explanation: Building load gradually this week — foundation before intensity.
Peak training stress this week — expect to feel challenged.
Reduced load to allow adaptations from earlier sessions.
Steady state — maintaining fitness without added stress.

josi_explanations:monotony_guard_rules.explanation: Session variety added to reduce repetitive stress.
Different energy system targeted to balance training load.
Load distributed across training types to prevent overuse.

josi_explanations:stimulus_cost_ratio.explanation: Efficient training: strong adaptive signal with limited fatigue.
Balanced session: stimulus matches the cost.
This session costs more than it builds — that's intentional for durability.
Heavy session: significant fatigue investment for long-term gains.

josi_explanations:zone_purpose_rules.what_it_builds: Active recovery — promotes blood flow without training stress.
Base aerobic capacity — foundation for all other work.
Aerobic efficiency — builds fat oxidation and mitochondrial density.
Lactate clearance — improves ability to sustain moderate intensity.
Lactate threshold — sustains hard, controlled effort at threshold.
VO2max development — increases oxygen delivery to muscles.
Anaerobic capacity — short, intense power development.
Speed and power — neuromuscular recruitment and efficiency.
Max power — peak force production and neural drive.

josi_personas_v1:frame_templates_balance.frame: Your body is responding well. A good day to build fitness.
Moderate readiness today. Keeping intensity reasonable.
Recovery is building. An easier session will serve you well.
Your body needs rest. Easy movement only. Recovery drives adaptation.

josi_personas_v1:frame_templates_technic.frame: Readiness indicates full recovery. Autonomic balance optimal.
Partial recovery. Autonomic metrics suggest moderate load appropriate.
Elevated fatigue markers. Reduced parasympathetic tone. Light work only.
Significant fatigue accumulation. Recovery session to support adaptation.

josi_personas_v1:why_templates.template: Your body is responding well. {zone} work fits perfectly for {mood} mood.
Moderate readiness. {zone} keeps intensity appropriate while matching your {mood} request.
Your body is responding well, and {zone} is the most valuable work for you today.
Moderate readiness. {zone} keeps today productive without overreaching.
Recovery is building, so keeping it lighter. {zone} is a smart choice.
Rest mode. Easy {zone} work supports recovery without adding stress.
Calibration {probe_seq} of {probe_total}: the engine is learning your patterns through five structured workouts. Today's {probe_type} probe shows how you respond, so every read becomes personal.
Recovery is building, so keeping it lighter. {zone} is a smart choice.
Rest mode. Easy {zone} work supports recovery without adding stress.
Another option at {zone}. Different stimulus, same quality session.
An easier alternative. Sometimes the smart session is the gentle one.
Building your baseline. Keeping intensity conservative while we learn your responses.
Being conservative while we calibrate. Better to underdo than overdo early on.

load_monitoring:policy_resolution_order.rule: If trust_level == low and readiness_level == Red → allow Z1_Z2 only (downgrade to Orange behavior)

load_monitoring:readiness_levels.description: Good fatigue — functional overreaching, restrict top zones
Danger zone — non-functional overreaching, recovery priority
Sick or severely overreached — hard stop, rest only

load_progression_interpretation:complian.josi_language: "a few sessions missed, but still solid"
"been tough to get sessions in — what's going on?"
"this week has been light — let's talk about what works for you"

load_progression_interpretation:load_int.josi_language: "your training load is building at a healthy rate"
"you've been doing less than usual — if that's intentional, fine"
"you've ramped up quite a bit — be mindful of recovery"
"your load has spiked — consider an easier day soon"
"stepping it up a bit — good, just watch how you feel"
"big jump from last week — make sure you're recovering"
"lighter week than usual — is that planned?"

load_progression_interpretation:load_sum.meaning: Total load from the prior week

load_progression_interpretation:load_vs_.josi_language: "a bit under the plan, but close"
"lighter week — your body might be telling you something"
"way below plan — let's check in on how you're doing"
"you've done more than planned — keep an eye on recovery"
"significantly more than planned — ease off to protect your progress"

mental_viterbi_emission:mental_viterbi_e.notes: contribution to fatigue likelihood (0..1); deliberately **below** decoupling's 0.4 so a single new subjective axis cannot out-vote the physiological emissions
semicolon-separated state:weight pairs (z-score means); max abs pull 0.5 ≤ decoupling's 0.6 (magnitude cap)
require at least 3 eligible `mental_state` observations in the window before contributing
VAS (1–10) value mapped to z = 0; inverted map `z = (vas_neutral − mean_vas) / vas_per_sd`, so a value *below* neutral yields positive (fatigue-direction) z
VAS points per standard deviation in the z-map

meso_dance_policy:ramp_in_rules.description: First 2 training sessions must be easy

monitoring_v5:readiness_levels.description: Good to train, all zones available

monitoring_v5:surprise_detection.window_days: y_1:t-1), indicates model was surprised by observation

onboarding_profile:derived_fields.rule: start_date.weekday() — meso day 0 aligns with plan start
Weekday pattern repeated across meso: Mon=0,7,14...
Complement: all meso days NOT in meso_train_days
Sum of all per-day minutes across the meso
Sum of weekday minutes / 60 (display only, engine uses meso_minutes)

onboarding_profile:optional_fields.notes: Cycling only. From test or device. Enables power zone targets.

onboarding_profile:optional_fields.notes: Running only. From test or device. Enables pace zone targets.
onboarding_profile:optional_fields.question: Threshold pace?

onboarding_profile:profile_to_engine.what it controls: Age modifiers, recovery scaling, meso_length, zone ceiling
Pattern complexity, protocols, dose envelopes, density, zone unlock
Recovery mult, rest mult, session caps, ramp limits, zone ceiling
Sport-specific recovery, zone ceiling, goal options
Phase sequence, zone distribution, archetype selection
Per meso-day availability, training day placement
Experience modifier, complexity penalty, adaptation rate

Inactive / just starting

"Need at least 2 training days per week"

planner_policy_v4:default_values.notes: Used when event type not found
Gabbett ACWR safe zone; <10% variation negligible

power_zones_cp:cp_anchor_priority.precondition: power_profile.source = onboarding_test or refit; r_squared >= cp_protocol.r_squared_min

power_zones_cp:cp_anchor_priority.use_case: Cycling when CP not yet tested but FTP exists
Cycling fallback before any power-profile is established

power_zones_cp:zone_purpose_cp.training_intent: peak sprint power, maximal neural drive

recovery_policy:recovery_pool.notes: Alactic neuromuscular touch permitted during active recovery — ATP-PC, ~zero metabolic load (Meeusen 2013 active recovery; Casado 2022; Barnes & Kilding 2015). Scoped exception to the Overreached×Z8→0.00 block above. Orange only, never red.

recovery_v4:same_zone_intensity.notes: pure alactic recovers fast, but the planner enforces 72h (standard) / 48h (micro) Z8 spacing per session_rules:zone_spacing_hours + energy_systems neuromuscular gap; low-volume alactic only repeats sooner in micro context

session_budgets_v4:quality_priority.rationale: Highest recovery cost, needs biggest slots

session_rules:day_to_day_ramp_limit.rationale: Gabbett 2016: >50% daily spike increases injury risk
Shorter sessions tolerate higher relative jumps

session_rules:phase_readiness_floors.rationale: Base: use standard scalar, no floor
Build: accept some fatigue, don't restrict too much
Peak: same as build, training must continue
Taper: standard scalar, let fatigue clear
Build: floor at 0.85, keep intensity available

session_structure_variants_v4:zone_varia.label_template: 3×3×20s @Z7 cluster (short rest in cluster, long between)

viterbi_advisories:acwr_recommendations.recommendation: Increase training load to maintain fitness
Load spike detected - high injury risk, reduce immediately

viterbi_advisories:ceiling_insights.template: Ceiling adjusted for {weeks} weeks of reduced activity
Ceiling adjusted down - {load} ULS caused fatigue

viterbi_advisories:ceiling_recommendatio.template: Over ceiling by {delta} ULS - recovery needed

viterbi_advisories:confidence_advisories.message: Low confidence - more data needed for accurate assessment.

viterbi_advisories:monotony_recommendati.recommendation: High monotony AND strain - vary training and reduce load
Training too repetitive - add variety in intensity/duration
Training load and variety are balanced

viterbi_advisories:pattern_advisory_desc.template: Recovery lag detected: {lag_count}/{high_load_count} high-load sessions followed by readiness drop within 48h.
Ceiling has been {trend} for {weeks}+ weeks at {ceiling} ULS. Training stimulus may need adjustment to drive further adaptation.
Readiness has been below Green for {declining}/{recent} recent observations. Sustained fatigue suggests a recovery period is needed.
No training recorded in the last 3 weeks. Plan may need adjustment for the break.
{gap_count} gaps of 5+ days in the last 3 weeks (longest: {max_gap_days} days). Training frequency may not match the plan.

viterbi_advisories:reactive_alert_messag.template: Sustained fatigue ({danger_days} days {severity}, {nongreen_days}/5 non-Green) with load at {load_ratio_pct}% of ceiling. Recovery needed before continuing the plan.
Weekly load ({weekly_load} ULS) exceeds learned ceiling ({ceiling} ULS) with sustained high loading. Reduce volume to prevent overreaching.
EWMA ACWR is {ewma_acwr} (danger zone) with declining readiness. Load spike combined with fatigue signals — reduce load.
Training monotony ({monotony}) and strain ({strain}) both elevated over 7 days. Add variety and reduce volume.

viterbi_advisories:state_recommendations.recommendation: You're fresh and clear — nothing's holding you back today. Full intensity is available.
You're adapting well — this is where fitness gets built. Keep the rhythm and stay honest about recovery.
Fatigue is starting to stack up. A lighter session today keeps the progress without digging a hole.
You've pushed past the productive line — today is active recovery: easy Z1 movement only, nothing that adds load. Gentle blood flow protects what you've built.
Your body is flagging illness risk — rest completely today. This isn't the day to train.

viterbi_policy:confidence_thresholds.description: Minimum confidence to trust Viterbi state for gating
Days of data needed for confident baseline

w_prime_policy:w_prime_dose_caps.notes: first hard session of week can deplete up to 1.5x W' (across all intervals; reconstitution between)
second high-intensity session capped at 1x W'
minimum dose to maintain top-end without fatigue

w_prime_policy:w_prime_readiness_gate.notes: full battery required for above-CP sessions
most above-CP sessions allowed; advisor may downshift VO2 short

w_prime_policy:w_prime_recovery_modeling.notes: Bartram 2018 accelerated default (trained/elite); matches cp_protocol.tau_default_seconds. Skiba 2012 model τ is larger (~300–800 s)
rest at least 5 τ between sets for ≥99 % W' reconstitution
ceiling; longer rests indicate set boundary

workout_history_interpretation:pattern_d.condition: planned Z4+ but executed Z2-Z3, 2+ times

workout_history_interpretation:planned_v.josi_response: "Your body chose easier — that can be smart"
"Going harder than planned adds fatigue debt"
ask how they feel, not why they skipped
"Rest days build what training breaks down"

workout_history_interpretation:session_h.meaning: Primary zone of the session (R, Z1-Z8, OFF)
Actual duration in minutes (0 if skipped)
What the plan said for this day

workout_history_interpretation:token_bud.component: total ChatContext budget for new fields
